hints.text: **Monte Titano** (739 m) dominates the landscape, visible from almost everywhere — look for the **three towers** to confirm
Landscape: smooth **agricultural hills**, many small winding roads, lush Mediterranean vegetation

shared_with.note: Indistinguishable from the surrounding Emilia-Romagna/Marche hills.
shared_with.diff: Short white plates + Monte Titano → San Marino.

hints.text: Plates **short, white, blue lettering** — most European plates are longer with blue strips
hints.uniq_note: Within an Italian-looking round the short strip-less plates are the giveaway; Italian cars drive through constantly, so check several.

hints.text: Road signs & bollards **identical to Italy's**; many signs point to nearby **Rimini**; poles round concrete or wood, like Italy

shared_with.diff: Plates + yellow-burgundy chevrons + lime bins → San Marino.

hints.text: The **only country in the world with yellow chevrons + burgundy-red arrows**
Elaborate pedestrian crossings: **blue-painted crosswalks + hanging signs** — specific to San Marino
**Lime-green trash bins** particularly common (in Gen4 some replaced by black bins)
hints.uniq_note: Within the Italy/San Marino border zone the lime bins lean San Marino strongly.

shared_with.note: Italy's Emilia-Romagna/Marche border region doesn't typically use these lime bins, but shares everything else infrastructure-wise.
shared_with.diff: Lime-green bins + short strip-less plates → San Marino; ordinary bins + long plates → Italy.

hints.text: **Superstrada di San Marino** (Dogana → City of San Marino) = the **only divided road** in the country, lanes separated by metal guardrail
The **historic centre** is covered by a Gen3 **trekker camera**
